title: Institute of Judicial Administration (Students Performance Assessment) Regulations, 2003
title_en: Institute of Judicial Administration (Students Performance Assessment) Regulations, 2003

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Institute of Judicial Administration (Students Performance Assessment) Regulations, 2003.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these regulations, unless the context otherwise requires:— “ academic assessment ” includes assessment based on coursework, examination, field attachment or thesis; “ academic year ” means such. period relating to any course conducted by the Institute , as the Governing Council may determine; " Act " means the Institute of Judicial Administration Act, 1998; [ Act No. 3 of 1998 ] “ award ” means any award designated by Governing Council for grant or conferment upon students who qualify in accordance with these regulations; “ cheating in examination ” includes any form or kind of dishonesty or destruction or falsification of liny evidence or irregularity that may reasonably be linked with the examination in question and during the examination process, irrespective of whether such is discovered within or outside the examination room; and “ Committee ” means the Academic Planning, Examinations and Students Affairs Committee (APESAC) established under regulation 4 “ Council ” means the governing body of the Institute established under section 6 of the Act ; “ examination regularity ” means possession of unauthorized material during the examination process; unauthorized absence from the, examination; cheating during, the examination process of any act or conduct during the extermination designed to defeat or interfere with the smooth administration of the examination a particular subject; “ Institute ” means the Institute of Judicial Administration - Lushoto established under section 3 of the Act ; “ Minister ” means the minister responsible for legal and constitutional affairs. “ Principal ” means the Principal of the Institute appointed under section 10 of the Act ; “ student ” means any person enrolled as such for any course conducted by the Institute lasting for one academic year or more; “ unauthorised absence from examination ” includes going out of the examination room temporarily or otherwise, or staying out of the examination room for an unduly long period, without authorization or perrhission of the invigilator or one of the invigilators during the examination in question; “ unauthorized material during the examination ” includes possession of any written or printed material that is generally or specifically prohibited from being in possession of the candidate during the examination process; phones; radios; radio cassette players; computers; and any other material intended to defeat the purpose of the examination or which may be specified from time to time by the principal to be authorized material. Part II – Establishment of the committee and its functions

provisions.title: The Academic Planning, Examinations and Students' Affairs Committee (APESAC)
provisions.content: (1) There is hereby established a committee to be known as Academic Planning Examinations and Students’ Affairs Committee (APESAC) which shall consist of the following members; (a) the Principal who shall be the chairperson; (b) two members appointed by the council from amongst its members; (c) the Director of studies, who shall be the secretary; (d) one students representative; (e) the Deam of students; (f) the Registrar; (g) the representative from the National Council for Technical Education (NACTE); (h) the Chief Training, Research arid Statistics Officer (CTRSO) from the judiciary; and (i) the Secretary, Council of Legal Education (2) The chairperson mays with the prior approval of APESAC, invite any other member of the academic or administrative staff of the institute to attend at any meeting of the APESAC, if it is considered that presence of such member would be beneficial to the delibrations of APESAC.

provisions.title: Functions of the committee
provisions.content: (1) The Academic Planning Examinations and Students’ Affairs Committee shall, subject to the Act , be responsible to the Governing Council for the proper conduct of the programmes and students’ affairs at the institute. (2) Without prejudice to the. generality of subregulation (1) it shall be the responsibility of APESAC with the approval of the Governing Council, to advise on, revise, vary or cancel any academic programmes or matter relating to the student’s; affairs and set academic standards and those incidental thereto; for the assessment of the performance of students. Part III – Awards and mode of assessment by the Institute

provisions.title: Awards of the Institute
provisions.content: (1) The Institute shall have the power to grant the following awards:— (a) a Diploma for a successful completion of a course of study lasting more than one but not exceeding three academic years. (b) a Certificate for a successful completion of a course of study lasting one academic year ; (c) Certificate of Attendance for a successful completion of a course of study lasting less than one academic year . Provided that a fee to be prescribed by the Principal and approved by APESAC shall be charged for each Certificate or transcript to be issued by the institute. (2) Every award of the Institute shall be so titled as to refer to; the course of study the successful completion of which entitles the candidate to be granted the award in either First class, Second class-upper, Second class-lower and pass as follows: 75% and above First Class 65% - 74% Second Class - Upper 55% - 64% Second Class - Lower 40% - 54% Pass (3) Every certificate award of the Institute granted in pursuance of these regulations shall be of such shape, size and form as the Governing Council may determine. (4) The Governing Council may, with the prior approval of the minister, designated and set conditions for other awards of the Institute in addition to those established by this regulation.

provisions.title: Standard of assessment
provisions.content: (1) Every student in a diploma or certificate course shall be assessed in respect of every examinable subject with an exception of field attachment or research as follows:— (a) Coursework in form of tests and written assignments during the semester when the subject is taught, shall account for 50% of the total marks for the subject in the following components: (i) a mid-semester test 20% (ii) a timed test 15% (iii) a written assignment 10%; and (iv) seminar presentation 5% (b) Final examination for the, subjects taught during the semester shall account for 50% of the total marks for the subject. (2) Rules governing the conduct of examinations shall be as provided for under the First Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Practical field work and field attachment
provisions.content: (1) All students enrolled for the Institute programmes shall undergo practical training or "Field Attachment" and this shall be one of the components for the, assessment of students. (2) Alt students sponsored by the Judiciary shall undertake their field work in courts approved by the Committee for Field Attachment (COTA) before the commencement of such practical training. (3) Students who are hot sponsored by the Judiciary shall undertake their field work either' in Courts in any other places as may be determined by COFA from time to time (4) For the Diploma, in Law programme the practical field work shall be at least for a period of eight (8) weeks but shall not exceeding ten (10) weeks. The output shall be determined by COFA subject to approval by APES AC (5) For the Certificate in Law programme, the field work shall be for at least six (6) weeks hut shall not exceeding eight (8) weeks. (6) Topics to be covered in fieldwork by Certificate in Law students shall be as may be approved by COFA. Part IV – Conduct of examinations

provisions.title: Registration and -eligibility for examinations
provisions.content: (1) Every person registered as a student shall be deemed to have been registered for the appropriate examinations in the relevant course of study. (2) There shall be no special registration of candidates for examinations. (3) Every student shall be required to attain a minimum attendance of eighty percent (80%) of all the formal class-room periods scheduled by the Institute for the course of study. Any student who, without reasonable cause accepted by the institute; fails or refuses to comply with this regulation shall thereby be disqualified from sitting for the final examinations at the end of the semester during which non compliance occurred.

provisions.title: Non-eligibility for assessment
provisions.content: (1) Where a student is discontinued: from a course, of study on4 disciplinary grounds in accordance with the Institute Judicial Administration Lushoto (Students General Welfare, Conduct and disciplinary matters) By laws 2000 and the, execution of such discontinuance is not stayed, shall not be eligible for tiny form of assessment under these regulations.

provisions.title: Evaluation criteria of diploma and certificate programmes
provisions.content: (1) Every student pursuing a Diploma or Certificate in law programme shall be evaluated on the basis of two components for each subject as provided for under regulation 6 ,(1) (a) and (b). (2) Each Examinable subject shall have at least two credit points. (3) A candidate shall be considered to have passed the examination if he scores at least 40% for every’ examinable subject; (4) A candidate shall be considered to have failed a particular subject if he scores less than 20 out of 50 marks ip the semester final examinations. (5) The key for grading of all subjects including research and/or field attachment reports shall be as follows:— (a) 75 and above A (b) 65-74 B+ (c) 55-64 B (d) 40-54 C (e) Below 40 F (6) Any mark which is 39.5% and above but less than 40% shall automatically be rounded up to 40% which is a pass mark. (7) The key for determining, classes, for Diploma or Certificate courses shall base on, the mean average of all subjects examined including field worker research and the indicators shall be as follows:— (a) 75-100 A = 1st class (a) 65-74 B+ = 2nd class-Upper (b) 55-64 B = 2nd class-Lower (c) 40-54 C = PASS

provisions.title: Supplementary examinations
provisions.content: (1) A candidate who fails in not more than six (6) credit points in one academic year shall required to sit for supplementary examinations. (2) Supplementary examinations shall be conducted within such time as the Institute may determine and approved by APESAC, after the date of declaration of the overall years results.. (3) Where a candidate sits for supplementary examinations under this regulation but fails at such sitting shall be required subject to sponsorship confirmation to repeat the relevant year of study. Provided that no candidate pursuing a course lasting one academic year shall be allowed to repeat: Provided that no student shall be allowed to repeat one year of study more than once.

provisions.title: Special examinations
provisions.content: (1) Where a candidate pursuing any programme of study at the Institute fails to attend the whole or part of an examination under circumstances which are beyond his control may, subject to production of authentic evidence and the approval of the APESAC, be allowed to sit for special examinations in the Subject(s) he did not do or complete. (2) A candidate who sits for special examination(s) under this regulation shall be considered to be sitting for that examination for the first time.

provisions.title: Discontinuation from studies
provisions.content: (1) A Diploma or Certificate programme candidate who fails in more than Six (6) credit points during any part of the academic year shall be discontinued from studies. (2) A candidate who is discontinued from studies as provided for under sub-regulation (1) of this regulation may be considered for readmission two years after such discontinuation. (3) A candidate who is found guilty of examination irregularities as provided for under regulation 16 (1) shall be discontinued from studies. (4) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-regulation (3) any candidate who is discontinued by reasons of examination irregularities may be considered for re-admission five years after such discontinuation.

provisions.title: Appeals against examination result
provisions.content: (1) Candidates may appeal against examination results of the grounds of unfair marking wrongful computation. (2) Rules governing examination appeals shall be as prescribed in the First Scheme. (3) Appeals shall be, lodged in form of a letter stating grounds of complaints to the Registrar within 14 days after the announcement of the examinations results. (4) No appeal shall be accepted for processing unless a fee of ten thousand shillings is paid, which shall be refunded only when the appeal is successful. (5) Appeal result shall, be declared provisionally by the Institute subject to the approval of APESAC.

provisions.title: Examinations irregulaties
provisions.content: (1) The rules governing the conduct of examinations shall be as prescribed in the Second Schedule to these Regulations. (2) The Principal shall, with the approval of APESAC have power to amend or prescribe the rules governing the conduct of examinations from time to time and lay down measures to be invoked upon contravention of such rules. (3) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (1) , any candidate who is proved to have involved himself in any of the examination irregularities as defined under these regulations shall be discontinued forthwith from studies subject to the confirmation of APESAC.

provisions.title: Examinations committee
provisions.content: (1) There is hereby established an examinations Committee which shall consider any reported examination irregularity and decide the appropriate action for any person found responsible for or guilty of such irregularity. (2) The members of the Examinations Committee shall be— (a) the Director of Studies who Shall be a Chairperson, (b) the Heads of Academic departments; (c) the Registrar, (d) the Examinations officer, who shall be the Secretary. (3) The Committee may, at any of its Sittings co-opt any person who, in the Committed opinion, will be of help in its proceedings. (4) The Committee shall have the power to summon any candidate allegedly involved in any examination regularity . (5) The Principal shall report to APESAC all serious cases of examination irregularities.

provisions.title: Internal and external examination regulations
provisions.content: (1) Each Examination shall be conducted under the control of the Office of the Director of Studies or such other offices' of the Institute appointed by the Principal . (2) The Examinations committee shall propose and submit to the Principal a list of names of recognized academicians and professionals to be external moderators or examiners of the courses taught at the Institute ; (3) The Principal shall after receiving a list of academicians under sub-regulation (1) recommend to the APESAC for approval the required number of such moderators and, or examiners. (4) The appointment of the external examiners or moderators shall be limited to two consecutive academic years though such person may be reappointed after |two academic years. (5) The office of the director of Studies shall ensure that as soon as practicable all examination scripts are sent to the appointed external examiners for moderation and where required, the comments of the external examiners or moderators shall be communicated to the internal examiners. (6) Each external examiner shall prepare a report after marking all answer books for the subjects in the course.

provisions.title: Date of examination
provisions.content: All Examinations in a semester shall be held at a time to be determined by the Institute and approved by the APESAC, which shall normally be during the period approaching the end of each semester.

provisions.title: Release of examination results
provisions.content: The provisional results of every examination may be announced by the Institute through a procedure to be determined by the Principal while final results shall be announced by the Registrar after they have been approved by the APESAC.

provisions.title: Preservation of scripts
provisions.content: The Institute shall preserve the students scripts the purpose of reference of a period of two years, after which the scripts may be destroyed.

provisions.title: Postponement of studies
provisions.content: (1) Any student may postpone studies for reasons of proven continued ill health supported by a doctor’s medical certificate, or for any other reasons which in the opinion of the management of the Institute is strong enough to prevent one from pursuing studies effectively. (2) The period allowed for the postponement of studies is one academic year only.

provisions.title: Scope of application
provisions.content: Unless otherwise stated, these, Regulations shall not be construed to apply to students taking a course which ordinarily lasts for less than one academic year .

provisions.title: Graduation ceremony
provisions.content: There shall be held a Graduation Ceremony in every calendar year. The ceremony shall be held on such a date and place as the Governing Council may determine from time to time. First Schedule (Under Regulation 14) Ruler governing examinations appeals 1. Examinations appeals shall be lodged in form of a letter stating grounds of complaints to the Registrar within 14 days after the announcement of the examination result 2. On receipt of an appeal the Registrar shall submit the appeal to the Appeals Committee, which shall read and determine whether the conditions stipulated in Regulation 14 have been, fulfilled. 3. After reading through the appellants letter, the Appeals Committee may where it finds that any of the mandatory conditions in Regulation 14 was not fulfilled, dismiss the appeal forthwith. 4. For locals, which meet the mandatory conditions, set out in regulation 14 . The Committee shall have the power to adopt its own procedure to handle them and where necessary, it shall appoint another impartial internal/external moderator and summon any witnesses whom they consider useful in the matter. 5. The Appeals Committee shall have the power to require any appellant to bear the cost of any investigation into the matter. 6. The Committee shall base its decision which must be unanimous on its findings under rule 4 above. 7. When voting under rule 6 above is done twice and there are equal votes of two sides the verdict shall be in favour of the appellant. 8. The Appeals Committee chairperson shall send the Appeals results to the principal who shall declare them provisionally subject to the annual of APESAC. Second Schedule (Under Regulation 15) Rules governing the conduct of examinations 1. (a) Invigilators shall be present in the Examination room at least ten minutes before the commencement of the examination. (b) Candidates shall be allowed into the examination room at least ten minutes before the commencement of the examination and shall ensure that they take their right places. (c) During the ten minutes while all candidates are seated at their right places, the invigilator(s) shall make all necessary announcements including the following— (i) That there is an obligation by candidates' sign the Attendance Register, which shall be circulated by invigilators not later than 30 minutes after the Commencement of examination; (ii) That the candidates have the correct examination question papers; (iii) that the candidates have to read the inductions on the answer books and on the particular examination question papers; (iv) that the candidates have to write their examination numbers on the appropriate space on the answer books. 2. The internal examiner(s) of the appropriate subject shall attend in the examination room at the commencement of the examination to assist the invigilators clarify any issues that are not clear in the examination paper. 3. The basic aid which candidates are allowed to bring with them into the examination room are a pen, a pencil, a ruler, and an eraser Any additional aid shall be provided for in the instructions. 4. Invigilation shall not admit candidates to the examination room after half an hour from the commencement of the examination and allow them to leave the room before one hour has expired after the commencement of the examination. 5. A candidate leaving the examination room temporarily shall be checked before being allowed to resume the examination and his absence shall be supervised to the extent that is possible. 6. During the first half hour of the examination, students shall fill in the Attendance Register. 7. No candidate shall, during any part of the examination session, communicate with any other candidate in any manner except through the invigilator. 8. Copying from another candidate or from any other source, unless authorized in the examination instructions shall be construed as cheating. 9. Once a candidate is found with unauthorized materials, the invigilator shall ask the candidate to sign on the materials for confirmation that they belong to such candidate. 10. The candidate shall be informed that he or she has contravened the regulations and, unless the contravention involves cheating, the candidate shall not be prevented from continuing with his or her paper. 11. Where a contravention is committed a written report shall be sent to the examination officer who is the secretary to the Examinations committee, including full details of the alleged contravention. 12. The Examinations officer upon receipt of the report shall convene the Examinations Committee meeting which shall deliberate on the alleged irregularity and make the appropriate decision or recommendations. 13. Any candidate, involved in cheating shall immediately be discontinued from the whole examination. 14. Where any candidate is proved to have been in possession of or to have unlawfully copied from any source brought or found in the examination room at any stage of the examination process shall be discontinued forthwith from studies subject to the confirmation of the Academic Planning, Examinations and Students Affairs Committee (APESAC). 15. Notwithstanding the provisions of rule 10, in the case of an examination irregularity other than cheating the invigilator may, at his discretion take any action which, in his view, is necessary and reasonable under the circumstances. 16. The invigilator(s) shall not permit candidates to leave their places before their scripts have been collected: Provided that any candidate who wishes to leave the examination room before; the time Specified for the examination in question shall hand in his scripts to the invigilator before leaving the examination room. 17. A candidate may remove front the Examination Room only those articles he was authorized to take into the room at the Commencement of the Examination. 18. Having collected ail the scripts and a signed register of candidates, the invigilator(s) shall hand over the scripts, register and the report to the Examinations officer and sign the appropriate handover forms.
